compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

The code-review `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (`code_review_result_v1`) persists only the SURFACED
findings — the `blocking`, `advisory`, and `coaching` buckets — and omits the `dropped` and
`indeterminate` findings that Pass-3 produced. It also does not persist the numeric
`block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` each finding was judged against. So "the complete Pass-1
finding list" is not recoverable for a code review, unlike plan review.

## Dependency

This story **depends on 4e19** (plan-review lossless v2), which lands first in the stack and makes
the SHARED review kernel stamp the resolved boundary on every decided finding: `pass3_decide`
returns `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` and `pass3_over_findings` merges them, so every
code-review finding (via `code_review_decide`) already carries them by the time this story runs.
4e19 also adds those two fields (plus `evidence`/`scenarios`) to the plan-review `_slim`, so the two
sidecars **converge** in shape — this story does not introduce a field plan-review lacks.

## What

Extend the code-review sidecar to `code_review_result_v2`, pooling the four buckets the code-review
gate actually produces — `blocking`, `advisory` (surfaced), `dropped`, `indeterminate` — with each
finding's full Pass-2 `verification` and Pass-3 determination (`decision`/`priority`/…), plus the
resolved `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` (stamped by the 4e19 kernel change). There is **no
`overflow` bucket** in code review: unlike plan-review (which applies an advisory cap that spills to
`overflow`), `code_review_decide` partitions only into `{blocking, surfaced, dropped, indeterminate}`
and the code-review verdict schema has no `overflow` property.

## Scope

The `dropped`/`indeterminate` buckets are computed by `code_review_decide` but are **not** currently
forwarded to the terminal verdict — `code_review_coach` assembles only
`{verdict, blocking, advisory, coaching, coverage}`, and `apply_region_gated_floor` only appends its
own novelty-floor drops post-hoc. So the wiring must be extended:

- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` — in the `verdict` step's `with:` block, pass
  `dropped: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.dropped }}` and
  `indeterminate: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.indeterminate }}` (alongside the existing `blocking`
  and `surfaced`).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` — `code_review_coach` accepts `dropped`/
  `indeterminate` inputs and includes them in its returned verdict dict; update its
  `code_review_coach_input`/`_output` schemas to allow the two new (optional) arrays.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` — bump `SCHEMA` to `code_review_result_v2`; in
  `build_payload`, add `dropped`/`indeterminate` (norm-id-stamped, like `blocking`/`advisory`), each
  finding retaining its `verification`/`decision`/`block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled`; KEEP the
  separate `blocking`/`advisory` lists (so the surfaced reader is unchanged) and the change metadata
  (`change_id`/`revision`/`change_fingerprint`) + `deps`. `_latest_payload_with_ts` and
  `latest_code_review_result` accept BOTH `code_review_result_v1` and `_v2` (replace the
  `== SCHEMA` guard with membership in `{v1, v2}`), so old records still read and the surfaced
  reader keeps returning the `blocking + advisory` union.

Code-review findings carry no `evidence`/`scenarios` (those are plan-review Pass-1 fields); this
story does not persist them for code review.

## Enumerated consumers to update

- `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws4.py:210` asserts `payload["schema"] == "code_review_result_v1"`
  on a fresh emit; update it to `_v2` (the schema bumped).
- `docs/experiments/derive_code_review_threshold.py` is a jq/offline consumer that filters on
  `code_review_result_v1`; note that it should accept `_v2` too (or is left as historical — out of
  this story's code scope, flagged for the reader).

## Compatibility & rollback

`v1` code-review sidecars remain readable (new buckets absent → default to `[]`). The novelty-floor
reader that consumes surfaced findings keeps returning the `blocking + advisory` union, so
region-gating is unaffected. No backfill. Reverting bumps `SCHEMA` back to `v1` and stops writing the
`dropped`/`indeterminate` buckets; already-written `v2` records stay readable because the reader
still accepts both schemas (the reader change is independently safe to keep).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `build_payload` produces a `code_review_result_v2` payload that pools `blocking`, `advisory`,
      `dropped`, and `indeterminate`, each finding retaining its `verification`, Pass-3 `decision`,
      and resolved `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` plus a `norm_id`. Oracle: a unit test driving
      `build_payload` with a verdict containing all four buckets and asserting on the payload JSON.
- [ ] `code_review_coach` forwards `dropped`/`indeterminate` from the decide step into the terminal
      verdict dict. Oracle: a contract test calling the `code_review_coach` op with those inputs and
      asserting they appear in the returned verdict.
- [ ] `latest_code_review_result` reads both a `v1` and a `v2` code-review sidecar. Oracle: a unit
      test with one hand-written record of each schema.
- [ ] The novelty-floor surfaced-findings reader returns exactly the `blocking + advisory` union
      from a `v2` payload, unchanged vs an equivalent `v1` payload. Oracle: a unit test comparing the
      two.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

file_impact.reason: pool all buckets + thresholds into v2 payload

compiled_state.title: code-review sidecar -> lossless v2 (all buckets + thresholds)
